The '''Mandalorian combat suit''' was a model of [[Mandalorian armor/Legends|Mandalorian armor]] in use during the [[Old Sith Wars]].

==Description==
The Mandalorian combat suit was a full body armor.<ref name="KotORCG" /> Although it was the most basic [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] combat attire existing during the Old Sith Wars, it provided a formidable defense. Its mesh design was capable of absorbing a remarkable amount of kinetic force. The armor was not [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]]-proof, but protected the wearer from small amounts of energy.<ref name="TSL" /> In addition, this combat suit was extremely flexible, and provided a wide range of movement.<ref name="KotORCG" />
[[File:Mandalorian_Combat_Suit.jpg|thumb|[[Atton Rand]] wearing a Mandalorian combat suit (without helmet)]]

The Mandalorian combat suit included a [[Helmet/Legends|helmet]] with an internal [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]] and could be equipped with a [[Jetpack/Legends|jetpack]]. The armor was sealed and included enough oxygen to provide the wearer ten hours of breathable air in the vacuum of space.<ref name="KotORCG" />

==History==
The Mandalorian combat suit was the type of armor favored by Mandalorians before the beginning of the [[Mandalorian Wars]]. Hence, it was the most common armor worn by [[Mandalorian Crusaders]] during the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]]. The Mandalorian armors used by the Crusaders were unique and customized in anticipation of battlefield or environment requirements.<ref name="KotORCG" /> The style of the Crusader armors evolved throughout the [[Mandalorian Crusades]] but they remained heavily personalized by their owners until the disappearance of the Mandalorian Crusaders themselves.<ref name="THoMA">{{SW|url=news/the-history-of-mandalorian-armor|text=The History of Mandalorian Armor|int=The History of Mandalorian Armor|nolive=1}}</ref>

Later, Crusader armors were considered by [[Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders/Legends|Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders]] as the symbol of the individualism that caused the downfall of the Crusaders. During the Mandalorian Wars, almost every Crusader veteran finally joined the Neo-Crusaders and adopted the standardized [[Neo-Crusader armor|Neo-Crusader light armor]]. However, a few Mandalorians refused to change their armor and continued to wear their old models of armors. A Mandalorian combat suit was still worn by [[Demagol]] during the Mandalorian Wars.<ref name="KotORCG" />

Decades later, Mandalorian combat suits were still in use during the [[Dark Wars]].<ref name="TSL" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Mandalorian combat suit first appeared in the [[2004]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]''.<ref name="TSL" /> The ''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]'' later provided more information about this armor and identified the Mandalorian armor worn by Demagol in the [[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic (comic series)|''Knights of the Old Republic'' comic series]] as a Mandalorian combat suit.<ref name="KotORCG" />
